archive_data.question_text: A paper is folded and punched as shown below. How will it appear when unfolded?

explanation.english: This question requires visual analysis of the paper folding and punching pattern. When a paper is folded and punched, the unfold pattern depends on the number of layers and punch locations. Without the specific diagram, the exact answer cannot be determined from text alone.
explanation.tanglish: Ithu oru vizhithal vizhaippu seyyalaana kelvi. Paper fold seyyappattu punch seyyappattu irukkum. Nerukkappatta diagram illamal thiyagam solla mudiyathu.

archive_data.question_text: In a certain code language, 'A + B' means 'A is the daughter of B', 'A – B' means 'A is the brother of B', 'A × B' means 'A is the wife of B' and 'A ÷ B' means 'A is the father of B'. Based on the above, how is P related to T if 'P + Q ÷ R × S – T'?

explanation.english: Breaking down the expression: P + Q means P is daughter of Q. Q ÷ R means Q is father of R. So P is sister of R. R × S means R is wife of S. S – T means S is brother of T. Therefore, R is wife of T's brother (brother's wife). Since P is sister of R, P is sister of brother's wife, which means P is wife's sister.
explanation.tanglish: P + Q endrathil P enbathu Q-in magal. Q ÷ R endrathil Q enbathu R-in appa. Athendral P enbathu R-in sagodari. R × S endrathil R enbathu S-in manaivi. S – T endrathil S enbathu T-in sagodaran. Athendral R enbathu sagodararin manaivi. P enbathu avalin sagodari endru artham. Athendral P enbathu manaiviyin sagodari.

archive_data.question_text: DSXL is related to FUZN in a certain way based on the English alphabetical order. In the same way, GMUB is related to IOWD. To which of the following is TFON related, following the same logic?

explanation.english: The pattern adds +2 to each letter position. D(4)→F(6), S(19)→U(21), X(24)→Z(26), L(12)→N(14). Similarly for TFON: T(20)→V(22), F(6)→H(8), O(15)→Q(17), N(14)→P(16), resulting in VHQP.
explanation.tanglish: Prathyekam endral oro aksharamukkum +2 seyyappadugirathu. D(4) irundhu F(6), S(19) irundhu U(21) endru. Athanpadi TFON endrathukku: T(20)→V(22), F(6)→H(8), O(15)→Q(17), N(14)→P(16). Athendral VHQP.

archive_data.question_text: What will come in the place of the question mark (?) in the following equation, if '+' and '−' are interchanged and '×' and '÷' are interchanged? 19 + 55 × 5 − 7 ÷ 3 =?

explanation.english: After interchanging the operators: '+' becomes '−', '−' becomes '+', '×' becomes '÷', and '÷' becomes '×'. The new equation is: 19 − 55 ÷ 5 + 7 × 3. Solving: 19 − 11 + 21 = 29.
explanation.tanglish: Operators matra pattal: 19 - 55 ÷ 5 + 7 × 3 endru agum. Ganitham seiyyumbol: 19 − 11 + 21 = 29.

archive_data.question_text: Eight people are sitting in two parallel rows containing 4 people each in such a way that there is equal distance between adjacent persons. In row 1 – L, M, N and O are seated and all of them are facing south. In row 2 – P, Q, R and S are seated and all of them are facing north. Thus, each person faces another person from the other row. Q sits to the immediate right of S. Only two people sit between L and M. S faces L. The one who faces N, sits to the immediate left of P. R does not sit at any of the extreme ends. Who among the following faces O?

explanation.english: From the clues: Row 2 has S-Q-R-P (left to right) since Q is right of S and R is not at extreme and R is left of P. Row 1 has L-O-N-M (left to right) since S faces L, two people between L and M, and R faces N. Therefore, O (position 2 in Row 1) faces Q (position 2 in Row 2).
explanation.tanglish: Row 2: S-Q-R-P endru amarvu. Row 1: L-O-N-M endru amarvu. S edhire L, R edhire N. Athendral O edhire Q amarnthu irukkirar.

archive_data.question_text: What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series? 878 880 884 890 898 ?

explanation.english: The differences between consecutive terms are: 880−878=2, 884−880=4, 890−884=6, 898−890=8. The pattern increases by 2 each time, so the next difference is 10. Therefore, 898 + 10 = 908.
explanation.tanglish: Vithyasam 2, 4, 6, 8 endru sutruvadithu valarndhu varugirathu. Adutha vithyasam 10. 898 + 10 = 908.
